name: HMS Loch Glendhu (K 619)
events: 15 Mar 1945 HMS Unruly (T/Lt. D.J. Palmer, RNVR) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR), HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN) and HMS Aire (A/Lt.Cdr. L.A., DSC, RNR). (1)

16 Mar 1945 HMS Unruly (T/Lt. D.J. Palmer, RNVR) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first with HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR) and later with HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN) and HMS Aire (A/Lt.Cdr. L.A., DSC, RNR). (1)

21 Mar 1945 HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Mounsey (?), HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN), HMS Welcome (T/A/Lt.Cdr. W. Downing, RNVR) and HMS Felicity (T/A/Lt.Cdr. S.F. Harmer-Elliot, RNVR). (2)

22 Mar 1945 HrMs O 23 (Lt.Cdr. A.J. Schouwenaar, RNN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory with HMS Mounsey (?) and HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN). Also a practice attack was carried out during which HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR) served as the target. (2)

7 Apr 1945 HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Loch Alsh with ships of the 8th Escort Group which was made up of the frigates HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR, with the Commanding Officer of the group, A/Cdr. J.H. Eaden, DSC and two Bars, RN, on board), HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN), HMS Loch More (Lt.Cdr. R.A.D. Cambridge, DSC, RNR) and HMSAS Natal (T/Lt.Cdr. D.A. Hall, DSC, SANF(V)) (a fifth frigate of the Escort Group was undergoing repairs at Belfast and was not present). (3)

8 Apr 1945 HMS Vitality (Lt. W.T.J. Fox, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Loch Alsh with ships of the 8th Escort Group which was made up of the frigates HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR, with the Commanding Officer of the group, A/Cdr. J.H. Eaden, DSC and two Bars, RN, on board), HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN), HMS Loch More (Lt.Cdr. R.A.D. Cambridge, DSC, RNR) and HMSAS Natal (T/Lt.Cdr. D.A. Hall, DSC, SANF(V)) (a fifth frigate of the Escort Group was undergoing repairs at Belfast and was not present). (4)

9 Apr 1945 HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Loch Alsh with ships of the 8th Escort Group which was made up of the frigates HMS Loch Achray (T/A/Lt.Cdr. L.H. Stammers, RNVR, with the Commanding Officer of the group, A/Cdr. J.H. Eaden, DSC and two Bars, RN, on board), HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN), HMS Loch More (Lt.Cdr. R.A.D. Cambridge, DSC, RNR) and HMSAS Natal (T/Lt.Cdr. D.A. Hall, DSC, SANF(V)) (a fifth frigate of the Escort Group was undergoing repairs at Belfast and was not present). (3)

12 Apr 1945 German U-boat U-1024 was captured on 12 April 1945 in the Irish Sea south of Isle of Man, in position 53°39'N, 05°03'W by the British frigates HMS Loch Glendhu (Lt.Cdr. E.G.P.B. Knapton, RN) and HMS Loch More (Lt.Cdr. R.A.D. Cambridge, DSC, RD, RNR). The frigates attempted to tow her to port but the boat sank while underway on 13 April 1945.
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
